subject: Chemistry
options: Momentum and energy always remain conserved.
Pressure is the result of elastic collision of molecules with the container's wall.
Molecules are separated by great distances compared to their sizes.
All the molecules move in straight line between collision and with same velocity.
solution: Molecules move very fast in all directions in a straight line by colliding with each other but with different velocity.

solution:
Lesser the value of critical temperature of gases lesser will be the extent of adsorption.

Gas SO2 CH4
Critical temperature/K 630 190

The critical temperature depends on the strength of the intermolecular interactions that hold a substance together as a liquid.

SO2 has stronger intermolecular force of attractions than CH4, due to the more molar mass and non-zero dipole moment.

So, Activated charcoal adsorbs SO2 more efficiently than CH4. is correct statement but Reason statement is not correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Andrews noticed that at high temperatures isotherms look like that of an ideal gas and the gas cannot be liquefied even at very high pressure. As the temperature is lowered, shape of the curve changes and data shows considerable deviation from ideal behaviour.

Carbon dioxide remains as a gas only up to point b. 

At point b, liquid of a particular volume appears. Further compression does not change the pressure. Liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide coexist and further application of pressure results in the condensation of more gas until the point c is reached. At point c, all the gas has been condensed and further application of pressure merely compresses the liquid as shown by steep line. A point like d represents liquid state

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to Van der Waals equation,

(P + aVm2) (Vm - b) = RT

Where, Vm = molar volume of the gas

R = Universal gas constant

T = Temperature

P = Pressure

At point A 'a' is considerable and 'b' is negligible.

So, (P + aVm2) Vm = RT

PVm + aVm = RT

Compressibility factor (Z) = 1 - aVmRT

Hence, the correct answer is 1 - aRTV

subject: Chemistry
options: $33.6 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~H}_{2(\mathrm{g})}$ is produced regardless of temperature and pressure for every mole $\mathrm{Al}$ that reacts
$67.2 \mathrm{~L~H}_{2(\mathrm{g})}$ at STP is produced for every mole $\mathrm{Al}$ that reacts
$11.2 \mathrm{~H}_{2(\mathrm{g})}$ at STP is produced for every mole $\mathrm{HCl}_{(\mathrm{aq})}$ consumed
solution:
$2 \mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{s})+6 \mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Al}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})+6 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{g})$
For each mole of $\mathrm{HCl}$ reacted, $0.5$ mole of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas is formed at STP. 
$1$ mole of an ideal gas occupies $22.4$ lit at STP.
Volume of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas formed at STP per mole of $\mathrm{HCl}$ reacted is $22.4 \times 0.5$ litre.
Hence, (D) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to Dalton's law of partial pressures, the total pressure by a mixture of gases is equal to the sum of the partial pressures of each of the constituent gases. 

Pi=xiPPi= partial pressure of ith component.xi=mole fraction of the ith componentP = total pressure

Let WH2=40 g⇒nH2=402=20

WO2=60 g⇒nO2=6032=158

PH2=2020+158×2.2

=2020+1.875×2.2

=2021.875×2.2

=2.0114

≃2.01bar

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Dalton's Law states that the total pressure P of moist air is the sum of partial pressures of dry air Pa and water vapor Pv: P= Pa+Pv. Only the absolute pressure is the state parameter of moist air.

Pgas+PH2O=4

Pgas=4-0.4=3.6 atm

As volume is doubled, Pgasnew =1.8 atm

New Total Pressure =1.8+0.4=2.2 atm

subject: Chemistry
options: Straight line with a negative slope.
solution:
According to Boyle's law, $P V=$ constant
$\therefore \log P+\log V=$ constant
$\log P=-\log V+$ constant
Hence, the plot of $\log P$ vs $\log V$ is straight line with negative slope.
